info.title: Ashlr Stack CLI
info.summary: Machine-readable catalog of the Ashlr Stack CLI (and MCP tool surface).
info.description:
This OpenAPI document treats each `stack` CLI command as a GET operation for the benefit of AI agents, LLM editors, and documentation pipelines that want to reason about Stack programmatically. It is NOT served as a real HTTP API — Stack is a local CLI. Consume this file alongside `/mcp.json` (the MCP tool catalog) and `/.well-known/ai-plugin.json` (discovery manifest).

Source of truth: `packages/cli/src/commands/*.ts` (citty `defineCommand` calls), mirrored into `packages/site/src/lib/cli-ref.ts`. When a command changes there, update this file too.

servers.description: Documentation and discovery surface. The CLI itself runs locally via `stack` or `bunx ashlr-stack-mcp`.

tags.name: setup
tags.description: Scaffolding, provisioning, adoption of existing repos.

tags.name: management
tags.description: Day-to-day operations on a configured stack.

get.summary: Scaffold a .stack.toml in the current directory.
get.description: Creates `.stack.toml` (committed) and `.stack.local.toml` (gitignored). Offers an interactive template picker unless `--template` or `--noInteractive` is given. Warns (but proceeds) when Phantom is not on PATH — Phantom is only required for commands that touch secrets.

parameters.name: noInteractive
parameters.description: Skip the template picker; always create a blank .stack.toml.

get.summary: Import an existing .env into Phantom + .stack.toml.
get.description: Routes every secret into Phantom, then best-guesses which provider each belongs to and writes matching service entries into .stack.toml.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Path to the .env file to import.

parameters.name: dryRun
parameters.description: Print what would happen without writing anything.

get.summary: Detect providers used by this repo.
get.description: Inspects package.json, lockfiles, config files, and `.env.example` to detect providers already wired into an existing repo.

parameters.name: auto
parameters.description: After scanning, offer to run `stack add` for each detected provider interactively.

get.summary: Clone a git repo and auto-detect its Stack services.
get.description: Clones the repo, then runs `stack scan`. If the repo already ships a committed .stack.toml, prints next-step guidance instead.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: GitHub / git URL. Only https://, http://, ssh://, and git@host:org/repo forms are allowed.

parameters.name: dir
parameters.description: Optional target directory (defaults to repo name).

get.summary: Provision a service and wire its secrets + MCP entry.
get.description: Runs the provider's four-step lifecycle: login → provision → materialize → persist. Requires Phantom on PATH.

parameters.name: service
parameters.description: Service name (supabase, neon, vercel, …). Omit for interactive picker.

parameters.name: use
parameters.description: Attach to an existing resource by id instead of creating a new one.

parameters.name: region
parameters.description: Region hint for providers that need one (e.g. us-east-1).

parameters.name: dryRun
parameters.description: Preview; no network calls, no vault writes, no MCP edits.

get.summary: Remove a service from the stack.
get.description: Removes the service from .stack.toml, deletes its vault entries, and cleans up .mcp.json. Optionally leaves the upstream resource intact.

parameters.name: service
parameters.description: Service name. Omit with --all to remove every service in this stack.

parameters.name: all
parameters.description: Remove every service. Requires typing `remove all` to confirm.

get.summary: List services configured in this stack.
Show everything Stack knows about a configured service.
get.description: Prints provider, resource id, region, secret slots (with vault presence), MCP wiring, dashboard URL, and runs a fresh healthcheck.

get.summary: Show stack health at a glance.
Inspect the effective env-var map for this stack.
get.description: Has two subcommands: `stack env show` (which secrets are present in Phantom) and `stack env diff` (which declared secrets are missing).

get.summary: Show the service dependency graph for this stack.
get.description: Renders an ASCII tree grouped by category, with every secret slot annotated beneath its service.

get.summary: Verify every service is reachable and credentials are valid.
get.description: Exit code: 0 if all healthy, 1 if any service failed. With --fix, asks before re-running `stack add` for failing services.

parameters.name: fix
parameters.description: Attempt auto-remediation by re-running `stack add` for failing services.

parameters.name: all
parameters.description: Run doctor across every registered project on this machine.

get.summary: Run a command with Phantom's secret proxy active.
get.description: Thin wrapper over `phantom exec` so env vars with phm_ tokens get swapped for real secrets at the network layer.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: Command to run after the literal `--` separator.

get.summary: Push secrets to a deployment platform.
Open a service's dashboard in your browser.
Refresh OAuth / PAT credentials for a specific provider.
List or apply starter stack templates.

parameters.name: continueOnError
parameters.description: Keep going when a single service fails (apply only).

get.summary: List every curated provider Stack can wire up.
Manage the cross-project registry at ~/.stack/projects.json.
Check npm for a newer @ashlr/stack release.
get.description: Does not auto-install — prints an install hint.

get.summary: Emit shell completion for bash, zsh, or fish.
Scaffold CI integrations that run `stack doctor` on pushes + nightly.

StackToml.title: .stack.toml
StackToml.description: Committed shape of a project's stack. Per-clone instance data lives in .stack.local.toml.

template.description: Optional: template slug this project was scaffolded from.

services.description: Map from service slot name (usually the provider name) to its ServiceEntry.

ServiceEntry.title: Service entry
ServiceEntry.description: One wired service inside a stack.

secrets.description: Secret slot names written into Phantom.

mcp.description: MCP server id wired into .mcp.json, if any.

ProviderEntry.title: Provider
ProviderEntry.description: One curated provider Stack knows how to wire.

name.description: CLI-facing name (what you type after `stack add`).
